title: Hieracium gothicifrons
intro: Hieracium gothicifrons, also known as Gothic hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and Eastern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may contribute to its potential in treating various ailments, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium gothicifrons include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, although further scientific research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Hieracium gothicifrons is a species of plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which comprises approximately 300 species of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. The plant's classification is further divided into the family Asteraceae and the order Asterales. This classification places Hieracium gothicifrons among the flowering plants, specifically within the monocots. The plant's specific classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of its taxonomic group.
parts: Hieracium gothicifrons is a plant species, and like many plants in the Hieracium genus, it has been used in traditional medicine.
